posts.text:
Honoured to Present at Microsoft CAP IDC AI Conference! 

I had the opportunity to present at the Microsoft CAP IDC AI Conf, where I spoke about our work on Sherlock & the OCV AI Playground to analyse OCV customer feedback data more effectively.

It was a great experience to share learnings, showcase our approach, and connect with so many colleagues passionate about building responsible and impactful AI solutions.

A huge thank you to the organisers, reviewers, and everyone who joined the session. 
Always grateful for the support of my team—your collaboration made this possible.

Excited for the conversations ahead and to continue exploring what’s possible with AI at Microsoft! 

#Microsoft #AI #Innovation #TechCommunity #MicrosoftLife #AIConference

resharedPost.text:
Big news from Microsoft Ignite 2025 — External collaboration in Microsoft Teams just got a major upgrade. 🚀

Anyone who’s worked across organizations knows the pain:
Different tools, access issues, permission loops, endless “Can you request access?” messages. It slows everything down.

This week at Ignite, we introduced a set of updates that make working with people outside your organization feel as natural as working with your own team.

Here are a few things I’m personally excited about:
💬 Chat with Anyone
Start a conversation with anyone using just their email. No setup. No account creation. Just a simple message that works.
https://lnkd.in/gFBXFhsD

📄 Content Sharing with External Users
Share content with external participants as easily as you do internally — Teams takes care of access and permissions in the background.

🔔 Multi-Tenant Notifications
For people juggling multiple tenants and accounts, this is a lifesaver. Stay active and keep context without constantly switching.
https://lnkd.in/g-jR5Ptd

🔐 Trust Indicators
Clear identity cues that help everyone understand who’s in the conversation. Small detail, big impact on confidence and clarity.

A milestone made possible by the collaboration of teams across Microsoft. Proud of what we accomplished together — and grateful for the journey.

#MicrosoftTeams #MSIgnite #ExternalCollaboration #FutureOfWork 
#Innovation

posts.text:
I saw many engineers blaming the outage on Microsoft 🤦‍♂️ SWEs blaming without knowing the root cause is concerning.

It is not Microsoft, it is Crowdstrike who released an update for Windows that had a bug. The patch runs in Kernel mode to monitor system activity at a low level.

Because it was running in Kernel mode, the buggy code was trying to access an invalid memory location that triggered a panic and showed a Blue Screen of Death.

The name of the driver file that had the buggy update is "C-00000291.sys", deleting it fixes the issue, and unfortunately, this needs to be done manually.
 
Microsoft has nothing to do with it.

comments.text: People can see the frontend, not the backend what's happening behind the scene. accept it and consider we will avoid such an issue in future rather than blaming each other.  It's like if you throw stone to others it will fall back on you in either way.

posts.text:
Koo an indigenous Bharatiya company & Twitter alternative is scheduled to shut down. 

But this isn't Bharat's first loss. 

Hike Messenger was launched in 2012 to rival WhatsApp. 

It was way ahead of its time and had better features than WhatsApp. 

-) Offline Messaging: First of its kind, WhatsApp doesn't have this even in 2024.
-) Video Calling: Hike launched it in September 2016, whereas WhatsApp launched in November 2016.
-) Voice Calling: Hike- January 2015, WhatsApp- March 2015.
-) Hike Direct: Launched in October 2015, this feature allowed users to share files without an internet connection using Wi-Fi Direct. WhatsApp still does not have an equivalent feature.
-) Hidden Mode: Hike has had this feature since 2014, WhatsApp introduced it 10 years later and still doesn't provide any encryption.
-) Stickers: Hike was one of the first messaging apps to introduce various stickers, including regional ones.
-) Timeline and Stories: Hike had its version of Stories and Timeline updates before WhatsApp introduced the Status feature in February 2017.

Hike died because Indians have an inherent bias towards foreign products. 

We suffer from colonial hangover and a Made in USA product implicitly gets preferred over a Made in India product. 

According to me, Hike Messenger's death is one of our greatest losses in the product space. 

We had a mammoth opportunity to tackle Meta and we lost it. 

Agree?

#hike #whatsapp #koo

comments.text: Remember the times when there was no 4G? In my opinion whatsapp used to deliver messages much quickly when compared to hike especially when the fast speed data got exhausted.

posts.text:
Title: From Clicking to Being Clicked: A Journey of Pride and Inspiration

Last week, as I embarked on a journey from Hyderabad to Pune in my Skoda Slavia sedan, little did I know that a simple stop for lunch would turn into a heartwarming encounter that would leave me inspired.

As I pulled into a hotel for a quick bite, I noticed two young boys admiring my car, their faces illuminated with awe as they snapped pictures. Stepping out after my meal, I caught them in the act, their initial excitement replaced by apprehension at being caught. But instead of shooing them away, I approached with a smile, inviting them to continue capturing the moment.

To my surprise, they confessed that they weren't just enamored by the sleek lines of my car; they were also inspired by the possibility of owning a similar one someday. Their enthusiasm was infectious, and soon their family members joined in, sharing in the joy of the moment and capturing memories with my car.
It was a poignant reminder of my own journey. I couldn't help but reminisce about my college days when I eagerly posed for a picture with someone else's car, dreaming of the day when I'd have one of my own. Now, as others stood beside my car, expressing their aspirations, I felt a surge of pride and gratitude.
This encounter served as a powerful reminder that dreams are within reach for those who dare to chase them. It's a testament to the transformative power of ambition and hard work. As I drove away, I carried with me not only the memories of that encounter but also a renewed sense of purpose.

resharedPost.text:
World's first Level-5 autonomous driving capability demo, where our autonomous vehicle learned to negotiate complex-traffic dynamics in the Toll-Plaza and learned to cross highly unstructured toll-gates. 

This is second only to our Nov-15 2017 and October 22 2023 demos, in terms of Level-5 capabilities, where we enabled #autonomousdriving for negotiating tight-stochastic-dynamic-adversarial environments via multi-RL agents, and bidirectional traffic negotiation on single lane roads, respectively. 

In pursuit of our Level-5 we started asking very tough questions: Can autonomous vehicles learn to cross toll-gates, that may be as unstructured as the ones in India, while also negotiating complex traffic-dynamics in toll-gate areas?

In this demo we showcase for toll-gate crossing and complex traffic negotiation at such intersections, while dealing with the highly stochastic behaviours of large trucks. This is the world's first and the only demo of #autonomousvehicles learning to perform maneuvers of such a complexity.

Our vehicle can be seen entering the toll-gate region through a highway, and then negotiating bidirectional traffic dynamics in an open area with literally no driving rules to adhere to in such regions. Truck drivers often park their trucks randomly at night in Toll-Plaza areas, which can be seen in the video, with numerous trucks parked there. Furthermore, our vehicle had to decide which toll-gate-passage to commit to, while negotiating the criss-cross navigation pattern of the trucks that over-took us.

To understand the level of complexity, a tractor tyre was placed behind a very-large 18-wheeler truck, the detection of which is very tough at night, and our vehicle successfully negotiated it, while a truck was overtaking us from the right.

Finally, our vehicle slowed and paused while approaching a speed-breaker and aligned itself with the toll-gate it committed to on-the-fly, and then crossed the toll-gate while adhering to the driving rule and speed limit at the toll-gate. Furthermore, the gate immediately left to us was barricaded with a broken traffic-police barricade, and our system detected it and adapted accordingly. 

We had developed a very-specific sophisticated motion planning and decision making algorithmic framework to execute this behaviour, and this framework is being further scaled with unsupervised deep learning, and in the month of February we will show an end-to-end negotiation of the day-time traffic.

#reinforcementlearning #deeplearning #machinelearning

posts.text:
With two consecutive news in the EV and autonomous vehicle industry first
1]BYD a big player from china overtook TESLA for its higher sales of electric vehicles  for year 2023.
2] Swaayatt Robots a Indian startup from Bhopal has successfully demonstrated world's first Level 5 autonomous driving capability.
They have automated  "Mahindra Bolero" by enabling  ADAS technology in it and demonstrated LEVEL 5 ADAS feature of crossing a Toll-Plaza successfully.
You can check the demo here:
https://lnkd.in/dQ5CunYM

So Lets Decode the Levels of ADAS:
What is ADAS?
Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS) are technologies designed to enhance vehicle safety by assisting the driver in various tasks. These systems use sensors, cameras, radar, and other technologies to monitor the vehicle's surroundings and provide warnings or take corrective actions when necessary. ADAS can be categorized into different levels based on the level of automation and the extent to which the system can control the vehicle. 

Level 0 - No Automation
At this level, there is no automation, and the driver is fully responsible for controlling the vehicle.
Level 1 - Driver Assistance
Level 1 systems provide a single automated system, such as adaptive cruise control (ACC) or lane-keeping assistance.
The system can either control the vehicle's speed or steering, but not both simultaneously.The driver is required to remain engaged and monitor the vehicle's surroundings.
Level 2 - Partial Automation
Level 2 systems offer a combination of automated features, such as adaptive cruise control and lane-keeping assistance, which can work simultaneously.
The driver is still required to monitor the environment and be ready to take control at any time.
(Tesla's Autopilot is an example of a Level 2 system)
Level 3 - Conditional Automation:
At this level, the vehicle can perform certain driving tasks autonomously under specific conditions.
The driver can disengage from actively controlling the vehicle, but must be ready to take over when the system requests.
(Audi's Traffic Jam Assist is an example of a Level 3 system)
Level 4 - High Automation
Level 4 systems are designed to perform specific driving tasks autonomously without driver intervention in certain scenarios.
The vehicle can operate autonomously within defined conditions, such as highway driving or urban environments.
However, the driver may still need to take control in certain situations.
Level 5 - Full Automation
Level 5 represents full automation, where the vehicle is capable of performing all driving tasks autonomously without any human intervention.
There is no need for a steering wheel or pedals, as the vehicle is fully self-driving.
Level 5 autonomy envisions complete driverless transportation.

Congratulations Swaayatt Robots (स्वायत्त रोबोट्स) for this great milestone.

posts.text:
Dear Recruiters,

Warm greetings from PUCSD!!

We, Placement Cell, Pune University Computer Science Department, Pune are glad to announce the inception of outreach for placement session 2023-24.
The Placement Cell is gearing up for a great recruitment cycle later after this month.

Department of Computer Science, Pune University is identified as one of the earliest Computer Science Department in India. In year 2023 PUCSD complete it's 43 years of excellence in the field of Computer Science shaping the minds which can change the world, who are ever ready to face the complexities of Dynamic corporate environment.
Our Alumni are working at Major organizations such as Amazon, Google, Microsoft, Nvidia, Barclays, Citi, Accolite, Commvault, IBM etc.

We strongly believe that the placement process is not just a yearly affair, but is an ongoing alliance between the institute and the organizations. We would like to thank the industry for its kind patronage and successful association with us year after year and look forward to similar "tie-ups" in future. We have persistently met with the ever-rising standards of the industry with suitable changes in the curriculum and culture. Our regular recruiters include Nomura, Commvault, Nvidia, Accolite , SAP and Agrostar.
Department is ready with 100 + brilliant minds pursuing MCA and MSc CS for the placement drives.

MCA and Msc students graduating in 2024 will be available for final placements (full time job offers).

MCA students will be available for 4 to 6 months compulsory internship in their final semester and they can be converted to full time employees if company is offering PPO to them.

We cordially invite you for Campus Placement Drives for Full Time as well as 4-6 month internship roles for batch of 2024.

For any further details please contact:

resharedPost.text:
Why "Work From Home" is more productive for individual contributors?
1. Transition to work zone is instantaneous as opposed to 1 hours involved in getting ready, booking a cab, and walking to your office desk.
2. Lunch times have greatly reduced as you have skipped the "lunch walk" or "lunch table tennis"
3. Most of us have setup office desk / room with better focus as opposed to office noise / chit-chatting.

The exception here is when you want to brainstorm ideas with team members which is easy and more enjoyable in office meeting rooms with a blackboard.

Follow #joinrachitonyoutube for more posts on #softwareengineering

UPDATE*: adding my reply to whether WFH is good?
WFH is more productive. Working in office is more fun and socializing :) 
Its noteworthy to mention its an extra effort on our part to keep mental sanity in WFH.

comments.text: I think wfh and weekly meeting at office might be perfect choice for software guys. 😅😅😅

author.occupation: Software Engineer

comments.text: Companies going with 3-4 wfh days and rest days in office model is pointless. It still means you have to rent a place in a different city, keep a maid and you can't live in your hometown. So the whole point of wfh goes to toss with this model. An ideal model would be to go to office for 3-5 days in a month or two.

Seems like those options are too far-fetched still, though...

posts.authorHeadline: Senior software engineer (SDE-III) at Microsoft | Ex-Adobe | Ex-Amazon

posts.text:
All great things come to an end!!

Today is my last working day with Adobe!! Spent wonderful 5+ years with this great company.

Coming from a small village and being completed most of my education in government schools/colleges(Marathi medium), I never thought of working for a company like Adobe (and previous company Amazon).

Got to learn so much both personally and professionally, spent some of the best time of my life, made some of the best friends and got inspired by some of the best brains in the industry which will be there for life!

With that I want to say goodbye to this wonderful journey with Adobe!  Hope our path crosses in the future!!

Excited for the journey ahead!!

#Gratitude #Adobelife #Adobe
